It does not generate tests, run RED-GREEN loops, spawn agents, or commit files.

GSD2 migration apply stages markdown/text evidence under .planning/imported-gsd2/** and writes a bridge ledger after --confirm-apply.
--write is a read-only preview alias and never overwrites existing targets.
Automatic conflict resolution, canonical state transition, shell execution, and external services remain out of scope.

Supported for bridge-owned workstream pause: validates active bridge metadata, rejects terminal workstreams, is idempotent when already paused, and by default writes only .planning/.bridge/workstreams.json. Explicit --save-handoff additionally writes .planning/HANDOFF.json, .planning/.continue-here.md, and .planning/.bridge/handoff-ledger/current.json. Native WIP commit behavior remains out of scope.
Adapted-safe because pause-work fails closed on missing or malformed bridge-owned metadata, writes only bridge-owned workstream metadata, and does not mutate .planning/STATE.md.
Milestone lifecycle, broad workstream lifecycle, and autonomous behavior remain pending coverage.

flags.name: --last
notes: Shows recent git commits as read-only undo candidates; with --apply --confirm-local-git requires exactly one candidate.

flags.name: --phase
notes: Selects all matching phase commits and can apply a confirmed no-commit git revert batch.

flags.name: --plan
notes: Selects all matching plan commits and can apply a confirmed no-commit git revert batch.

flags.name: --apply
notes: Applies git revert --no-commit only with --confirm-local-git and explicit scope.

flags.name: --confirm-local-git
notes: Required safety confirmation for local git revert mutation.
Bridge undo now performs exact-safe local git revert behavior behind --confirm-local-git.
Dry-run mode remains read-only and previews recent, phase, or plan-scoped candidate commits.
Confirmed apply mode uses git revert --no-commit for the selected --last single commit or all matching --phase/--plan commits, leaving final commit/abort decisions to the operator.
Reset, checkout, history rewrite, remote push, and interactive confirmation prompts remain out of scope.

flags.name: --next
notes: Selects the @next release-candidate channel for readiness guidance without package install.

flags.name: --rc
notes: Alias for --next.

flags.name: --sync
notes: Refreshes bridge-owned install-managed target project surfaces through the repair lifecycle, preserving local edits as pending updates or local patches.

flags.name: --reapply
notes: Renders deterministic local patch reapply guidance without applying custom patch hunks automatically.
Bridge update now supports exact-safe managed-surface sync through the repair lifecycle.
Standard/latest/next modes remain read-only readiness reports and do not query npm or install packages.
--sync refreshes bridge-owned installed surfaces, updates install-state hashes, and preserves divergent local edits as pending updates or local patches.
--reapply remains guidance-only; local patch hunk reapplication is still explicit operator work.

flags.name: --apply
notes: Validation artifact repair and test generation remain blocked in the bridge adapter.
Bridge validate-phase is adapted as a read-only validation evidence and gap-marker report over .planning/phases.
It distinguishes validation-present, summary-only, phase-not-executed, and no-phase-found states.
It does not write VALIDATION.md, generate tests, execute validation commands, or update planning state.
Current bridge entrypoint has a packet path; audited reference parity gaps remain explicit at flag level.
Bridge implements workspace inspection and workspace select behavior backed by bridge-owned .planning/.bridge/workstreams.json metadata.
Adapted-safe because workspace selection writes only bridge-owned metadata, reports previous active workspace evidence, and does not mutate .planning/STATE.md.
Vendor workspace modes beyond inspect/select remain pending future parity slices.
Bridge implements deterministic workstream inventory and workstreams select behavior backed by bridge-owned .planning/.bridge/workstreams.json metadata.
Adapted-safe because workstreams select requires an active workspace, writes only bridge-owned metadata, reports previous active workstream evidence, and does not mutate .planning/STATE.md.
Exact vendor workstream lifecycle modes, milestone lifecycle, and autonomous behavior remain future parity work.
